what_it_does: Defines a design DNA manifest with project identity, version, generated source files, consumed contract text, layer file locations, derivation notes, and release notes.
observations: The manifest name is "design-dna" and version is "0.8.0".
The identity is stated as "warm · premium · architectural".
The generated_from list names eight dna/*.json source files.
The layers object maps tokens, templates, components, icons, application, vocabulary, modes, motion, sequence, voice, spaces, invariants, and dials to output JSON paths.
The consumes_contract string names DTCG tokens, component.schema.json template/molecule types, and design-lint predicates.
The derivation note describes ratio-based space/radius/type, scale-free proportions, ordinal colour sampling, and sequence resolution counts.
The notes field describes a 0.2.0 registry circuit addition and says there are 11 invariant predicates.
standouts: It is a manifest-level ledger rather than a data layer itself.
It records both source inputs and generated output layer addresses.
It includes generation rationale in prose fields, not only file references.
conventions: Uses nested objects for grouped layer families such as vocabulary, spaces, and dials.
Uses arrays for multi-file layer groups such as templates and components.
Keeps paths as relative JSON file strings.
Combines machine-readable structure with dense human-readable prose in contract, derivation, and notes fields.
concerns: The manifest version is 0.8.0 while the notes field specifically describes 0.2.0, leaving the current-version note unclear.
The relative path base for generated_from and layer paths is not stated in the file.
The consumes_contract and derivation fields pack several separate facts into single prose strings.
notes: Briefing note: this file is the design DNA manifest that ties identity, generation inputs, output layers, contracts, and derivation notes together.
questions: Are generated_from paths and layer paths relative to the same directory root?
How does the 0.2.0 note relate to the current 0.8.0 manifest version?
Are the 7/7, 11/11, and 11 invariant counts expected to be validated elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it functions as a manifest for design DNA identity, inputs, output layers, contracts, and derivation notes.
apparent_themes: sequence and motion as first-class layers
intention_signals: It appears intended to make the generated design system understandable and traceable by recording what produced it, what it exposes, and what contract it expects consumers to follow.
novelty: It frames a design system manifest as a DNA ledger with grammar, molecules, motion, sequence, spaces, voice, invariants, and dials all addressed together.
contribution: Contributes a manifest record of design identity, source inputs, layer addresses, consumption contract, derivation rules, and release context for a fused system to retain as orientation metadata.
summary_for_embedding: A design DNA manifest JSON file declaring versioned identity, generated inputs, output layer paths, consumption contract, derivation logic, and notes for counterpart-design.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for orienting consumers and tooling around the generated design DNA package and its layered visual-design outputs.
